commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=49b6ff2e11b626b832b8c3cbdf1e5957c96eda5e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch converts Doctor plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repair paths into structured health-check detection and repair results with changes, warnings, effects, diffs, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the disabled plugin-registry finding reaches repair without an explicit skipped status, while the runner defaults missing status to repaired and validates again afterward.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer handling plus contributor proof for a protected draft stack; automation cannot supply the contributor’s current-head real-environment proof.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Goal

Move plugin registry and configured plugin install repairs into the structured Doctor repair contract started by #83753.

This wave is about plugin repair policy that used to live in command-specific flows: detect the broken plugin registry or configured install state, pass those exact findings into repair, and report the repair as structured `changes`, `warnings`, `effects`, and diffs where file edits are available.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Legacy plugin doctor repairs mixed registry detection, configured install checks, prompting, and output text in the same flow. This PR keeps the existing behavior and contribution order, but makes the result isomorphic:

- lint and repair start from the same plugin findings
- repair only acts on the findings it receives
- dry-run can describe the plugin install/config changes without mutating state
- repair output records effects and warnings instead of relying on presentation text
- note text remains suppressible presentation, not machine signal

## Converted Here

- plugin registry doctor checks
- configured plugin install doctor repairs
- plugin manifest repair integration points touched by the registry flow
- focused plugin doctor tests around the converted paths

## Review Surface

This is the plugin-policy PR to challenge before the rest of the stack assumes the shape:

- whether plugin install findings are grouped at the right level: registry entry, configured plugin, or install root
- whether repair effects should identify plugin ids, package refs, or filesystem targets differently
- whether registry repair should expose more concrete diffs for config writes
- whether any plugin-specific policy is leaking into generic Doctor runner code

## Stack Position

Parent: #83753. This PR is the plugin registry/configured install wave in the Doctor repair stack.

Follow-on PRs continue the same contract through sandbox, gateway services, session state, config audit/cron, and finally the top-level dry-run preview surface.

## Verification

- Restacked on #83753 as a single commit.
- Code search confirmed this branch does not reintroduce `HealthPresentationNote`, `repairNote`, `detectionNotes`, `repairNotes`, or structured note result surfaces.
- Changed test coverage includes plugin registry, plugin manifests, doctor completion, health contribution, and repair-flow tests for this slice.
- Fresh PR CI should provide broad proof across the full stack.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin registry and configured plugin install repairs now use finding-scoped structured repair output while preserving existing plugin repair behavior.

Real environment tested: Windows checkout on the refreshed stack.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: no focused command completed locally for this PR body refresh; rely on PR CI for broad proof.

Evidence after fix: The branch updates plugin doctor implementation and colocated tests so the migrated repair paths report structured changes/effects/warnings and keep notes out of lint/JSON result payloads.

Observed result after fix: Plugin repair behavior remains available through Doctor, while dry-run/repair callers can consume structured repair output instead of presentation text.

What was not tested: Full plugin install scenarios and platform-wide Doctor CI were not rerun locally for this body refresh.
